description: Curated demo glaze collection for OpenGlaze. All recipes are based on classic, well-published ceramic formulations that are part of the public domain of ceramic chemistry knowledge. These are reference/formulaic glazes, not proprietary recipes.
source: Traditional ceramic chemistry, standard glaze formulation references, public-domain educational materials

glazes.name: Celadon
glazes.description: Classic celadon glaze. Iron-bearing reduction glaze producing soft green tones. One of the most iconic glaze families in ceramic history.
glazes.recipe: Custer Feldspar 45, Silica 25, Whiting 18, EPK 12, Red Iron Oxide 1.5
glazes.notes: Classic formulation based on traditional East Asian celadon chemistry. Iron content (1-2%) produces the characteristic green in reduction.

glazes.name: Tenmoku
glazes.description: Classic tenmoku glaze. High-iron reduction glaze producing deep brown to black with possible oil spot effects.
glazes.recipe: Custer Feldspar 50, Silica 20, Whiting 15, EPK 12, Red Iron Oxide 8
glazes.notes: Traditional Japanese/Chinese tenmoku based on published iron-rich glaze formulations. High iron (6-10%) creates the deep, lustrous surface.

glazes.name: Shino
glazes.description: Traditional Shino glaze. High-feldspar, low-silica formulation that produces warm orange, white, or carbon-trap effects in reduction.
glazes.notes: Traditional Japanese Shino based on high feldspar content. Unusual chemistry — very high alumina, very low silica. Produces warm, earthy surfaces.

glazes.name: Cone 6 Clear
glazes.description: Standard cone 6 oxidation clear glaze. Transparent, glossy, and reliable for functional ware in electric kilns.
glazes.recipe: Ferro Frit 3124 30, Custer Feldspar 25, Silica 20, EPK 15, Wollastonite 10
glazes.notes: Standard cone 6 clear based on common frit-based formulations. Boron from the frit ensures melt at lower temperatures.

glazes.name: Magnesia Matte
glazes.description: Cone 10 magnesia matte glaze. High dolomite content creates a soft, buttery matte surface. Excellent for functional ware.
glazes.recipe: Nepheline Syenite 30, Silica 5, Whiting 15, EPK 40, Dolomite 10
glazes.notes: Magnesia matte based on published alkaline earth matte formulations. High MgO from dolomite creates the matte surface through crystal formation.

glazes.name: Copper Red
glazes.description: Classic copper red glaze. Requires heavy reduction atmosphere to develop the famous sang de boeuf red.
glazes.recipe: Custer Feldspar 45, Silica 25, Whiting 15, EPK 10, Copper Carbonate 1.5, Tin Oxide 2
glazes.notes: Traditional copper red based on published reduction glaze chemistry. Tin oxide assists copper reduction. Requires careful reduction timing.

glazes.name: Nuka
glazes.description: Traditional Japanese Nuka glaze. Wood ash-inspired formulation producing a creamy, semi-opaque surface with subtle texture.
glazes.notes: Nuka is traditionally made with wood ash. This feldspar-based approximation captures the creamy, semi-opaque character.

glazes.name: Low-Fire White
glazes.description: Cone 04 opaque white glaze for earthenware. Reliable, bright white suitable for low-fire oxidation.
glazes.recipe: Ferro Frit 3124 50, EPK 25, Silica 15, Zinc Oxide 5, Tin Oxide 5
glazes.notes: Standard low-fire opaque white based on frit and tin oxide opacification. Widely used as a base for low-fire colored glazes.
